channel.title: Tech Blog - Acesyde
channel.description: Software Architect at Betclic Group, based in France. I write about .NET, Python, Rust, Docker, AWS and Azure — plus the occasional home-automation project.

item.title: How to Automate Your Gate with Home Assistant and ESPHome
item.description: Automating a CAME BX-243 gate with Home Assistant, a Shelly UNI and ESPHome — wiring, firmware flashing, and fully local, cloud-free control.

item.title: How to Automate Your Swimming Pool with Home Assistant
item.description: Automating a pool pump with Home Assistant, Zigbee (SONOFF ZBMini, OWON sensor) and a Node-RED flow that adapts filtration time to water temperature.

item.title: The Journey from Makefiles to Mise: Transforming Our Development Workflow
item.description: Why our team moved away from Makefiles and bash scripts to mise for consistent, reproducible development environments across repos and CI/CD.

item.title: How We Use Mise and DevContainers to Simplify Development
item.description: How we combine Microsoft DevContainers with mise for clean, reproducible dev environments — with a real-world devcontainer.json and setup scripts.

item.title: Sharing Tasks with Mise - How We Standardize and Scale Across Projects
item.description: How we share versioned mise tasks across projects and CI with a centralized repository and a download-tasks script for consistent tooling.

item.title: Dual-Booting Fedora and Windows: How to Sync Bluetooth Link Keys Without Leaving Linux
item.description: Fix Bluetooth re-pairing on a Fedora/Windows dual-boot by syncing the Windows Link Key into Linux — entirely from Linux, using chntpw and ntfs-3g.

item.title: Get mise.toml Autocomplete in Zed
item.description: A quick tip to get schema-backed autocomplete and validation for your mise.toml files in the Zed editor using the TOML and Tombi extensions.
